I've found relevant information about Nexus Mods' mod browsing overhaul. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## Mod Browsing Overhaul Overview

The redesign has been a significant piece of work, representing a big step forward for the site that updated fundamental parts of the website and content browsing experience that had been untouched for almost a decade.

## Design Goals

The new design improves the hierarchy of content, making it easier to browse across different sections on a page while standardising elements of the layout across different pages. Making the site easier to browse is intended to encourage more people to find and use mods, ultimately benefiting the whole community.

## Community Feedback

The response from the community has been mixed. The forum feedback thread on these changes has been viewed 280k times with 2k comments, and every comment has been read by the product, design and community teams, with most of them being not positive.

## Key Concerns & Responses

More experienced site users reported that the reduction in visible content feels like a significant loss, as grid and list views they preferred offered more content visibility, and the team is exploring additional tile options including a list view and a more compact view.

## Performance

Some pages have suffered performance issues requiring fine-tuning, though some pages are now on par with the old versions while others show improved performance.
